description:
You are a security engineer rotating the access credentials for a private
    APT repository that lives at repo.priv.example.com.  
    The previous (2023) credential and signing-key are still present on the
    system.  Your goal is to replace them with the new (2024) credential set,
    update every relevant APT configuration file, and leave an auditable log of
    what you did.

    Starting situation
    ------------------
    1. Old credential file  
       • Path: /home/user/.config/privrepo/creds.conf  
       • Contents:  
         token=OLD-TOKEN-2023

    2. Old repository signing key  
       • Path: /home/user/.config/apt/trusted.gpg.d/privrepo_old.gpg  
       • Contents are irrelevant to you; it just exists.

    3. The new signing key that must be installed  
       • Path: /home/user/keys/new_privrepo.gpg  
       • It is a small dummy file that pretends to be an ASCII-armoured GPG key.

    4. None of the following files/directories exist yet (create them where
       needed, including parent directories):
       • /home/user/.config/apt/auth.conf.d/privrepo.conf  
       • /home/user/.config/apt/sources.list.d/privrepo.list  
       • /home/user/.config/apt/preferences.d/privrepo.pref  
       • /home/user/.config/apt/trusted.gpg.d/privrepo.gpg  
       • /home/user/rotation_logs/2024-credential-rotation.log

    Credential to install
    ---------------------
    NEW-SUPER-SECRET-TOKEN-2024

    Required end-state
    ------------------
    1. The old credential file must be kept **as a backup** by renaming it to
       /home/user/.config/privrepo/creds.conf.old.  
       A brand-new /home/user/.config/privrepo/creds.conf must be created whose
       sole line is exactly (no leading/trailing spaces):

         token=NEW-SUPER-SECRET-TOKEN-2024

    2. APT *auth.conf* entry  
       New file: /home/user/.config/apt/auth.conf.d/privrepo.conf  
       Exact content (including the final newline and the two-space indents):

         machine repo.priv.example.com
           login token
           password NEW-SUPER-SECRET-TOKEN-2024

    3. APT *sources.list* entry  
       New file: /home/user/.config/apt/sources.list.d/privrepo.list  
       Exact single line (include trailing newline):

         deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/home/user/.config/apt/trusted.gpg.d/privrepo.gpg] https://repo.priv.example.com/stable/ focal main

    4. APT *preferences* pinning  
       New file: /home/user/.config/apt/preferences.d/privrepo.pref  
       Exact content (include trailing newline):

         Package: *
         Pin: origin "repo.priv.example.com"
         Pin-Priority: 900

    5. Signing-key rotation  
       • Copy /home/user/keys/new_privrepo.gpg to  
         /home/user/.config/apt/trusted.gpg.d/privrepo.gpg  
       • Do **not** delete the old
         /home/user/.config/apt/trusted.gpg.d/privrepo_old.gpg.

    6. Audit log  
       Create the directory /home/user/rotation_logs (if missing) and the file
       /home/user/rotation_logs/2024-credential-rotation.log with exactly the
       seven log lines shown below (each line must end with a newline).
       The log is plain text, *not* JSON:

         2024-credential-rotation:
           removed: /home/user/.config/privrepo/creds.conf (old credentials)
           created: /home/user/.config/privrepo/creds.conf (new credentials)
           updated: /home/user/.config/apt/auth.conf.d/privrepo.conf
           updated: /home/user/.config/apt/sources.list.d/privrepo.list
           copied: /home/user/keys/new_privrepo.gpg -> /home/user/.config/apt/trusted.gpg.d/privrepo.gpg
           created: /home/user/.config/apt/preferences.d/privrepo.pref

    Constraints & tips
    ------------------
    • Stay entirely in /home/user; you do **not** have root.  
    • Keep directory names and file paths **exact**.  
    • Preserve the precise indentation and spacing shown above—
      automated tests perform byte-for-byte comparisons.  
    • Use any approach you like, but the whole task can be completed in roughly
      5-10 shell commands (mv, cp, mkdir, echo/redirection, etc.).  
    • When you’re finished, verify that every required file exists with the
      specified content and that the audit log lines are exactly as shown.

    The automated grader will check:
      – that the backup file and new credential file both exist with correct content,  
      – that all four APT configuration files exist with *byte-perfect* content,  
      – that the new key was copied, the old key still exists,  
      – and that the audit log matches the required seven lines exactly.

</file>

</file>
        <file path="/home/user/.config/apt/preferences.d/privrepo.pref">Package: *
Pin: origin "repo.priv.example.com"
Pin-Priority: 900
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/.config/apt/trusted.gpg.d/privrepo.gpg">-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----
Version: GnuPG v1

new dummy key
-----END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/.config/apt/trusted.gpg.d/privrepo_old.gpg">-----BEGIN PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----
Version: GnuPG v1

-----END PGP PUBLIC KEY BLOCK-----
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/rotation_logs/2024-credential-rotation.log">2024-credential-rotation:
  removed: /home/user/.config/privrepo/creds.conf (old credentials)
  created: /home/user/.config/privrepo/creds.conf (new credentials)
  updated: /home/user/.config/apt/auth.conf.d/privrepo.conf
  updated: /home/user/.config/apt/sources.list.d/privrepo.list
  copied: /home/user/keys/new_privrepo.gpg -> /home/user/.config/apt/trusted.gpg.d/privrepo.gpg

</file>
